tests.title: Teen Cooking Project
tests.subtitle: Organisers describe how this cooking project developed.
tests.text: Teen Cooking Project began after several teenagers became interested in cooking. At first, meetings were organised in a small local centre during weekends. [11]The organisers originally expected only a few students to participate regularly[/11]. However, interest increased quickly after photos and videos were shared online. Students from nearby schools soon asked if they could join too. In the beginning, organisers had problems because there was little equipment and limited experience planning events. Even so, members enjoyed learning practical skills together and helping each other improve. After several months, experienced students started helping beginners during activities. [12]Organisers realised that newcomers improved faster when older students explained tasks clearly[/12]. Teachers and parents later noticed that participants seemed more confident and independent outside school. Some former members eventually chose university courses connected with cooking. [13]The organisers were surprised that the programme influenced participants’ future study decisions[/13]. Today, meetings usually begin with short presentations before students complete practical tasks in groups. Members are encouraged to ask questions and share ideas openly during sessions. Recently, organisers created online tutorials so students can continue practising at home. [14]The organisers believe regular practice is more important than expecting immediate success[/14]. Although the programme is now larger, organisers still try to create a relaxed atmosphere where new members feel welcome. Students are reminded that mistakes are a normal part of learning. [15]The main aim is to help young people feel confident trying unfamiliar experiences[/15].

questions.question: What did organisers expect when the project started?

options.A: Only a small number of students would join.
options.B: The project would become international quickly.

questions.explanation: Organisers originally expected a small group.

questions.question: What helped beginners improve more quickly?
Why were organisers surprised later?
questions.explanation: The project unexpectedly influenced future studies.

questions.question: What do organisers believe is important?
What is the main purpose of the programme?

options.A: Helping young people feel confident trying new things.

questions.explanation: The programme encourages confidence and participation.
